mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, with Rank IC below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of instances when UNRATE rises > 0.5pp over 3 months, contradicting the labor deterioration channel.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized strength in consumer demand and residential investment, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns when RSAFS and HOUST both rise month-over-month, with a Rank IC below 0.02 over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month expansions in the index of industrial production reflect immediate improvements in manufacturing output and capacity utilization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of months where INDPRO increases, regardless of the broader economic cycle or baseline volatility.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation without corresponding monetary tightening erodes real corporate margins and prompts market repricing, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises > 0.4% month-over-month while FEDFUNDS remains flat
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in > 60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds a 0.4% monthly increase while FEDFUNDS is unchanged, invalidating the margin compression channel.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show a negative or zero correlation with PAYEMS growth over a 3-year rolling window, with a Rank IC below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly retail sales confirms broad-based demand expansion, which sustains corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined GDPC1 and RSAFS growth signal predicts negative 60-day returns in > 50% of quarters, invalidating the demand transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in industrial production signal immediate supply-side disruptions and falling business confidence, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in > 65% of months where INDPRO drops by more than 1.0%, contradicting the supply shock transmission mechanism.
